title: Consumer Policy
summary: This is a landing page for the Treasury Department's Office of Consumer Policy (OCP), which promotes financial literacy, consumer education, and access to credit. The page describes OCP's coordination of the Financial Literacy and Education Commission (FLEC), links to annual reports, and references initiatives including Trump Accounts briefing and a national financial literacy strategy. The content is informational and programmatic in nature.
key_points: The Office of Consumer Policy leads Treasury's consumer financial education and literacy efforts
OCP manages the Financial Literacy and Education Commission (FLEC), chaired by the Treasury Secretary
A FLEC public meeting was held February 6, 2026 featuring a briefing on Trump Accounts implementation
The page links to FLEC Annual Reports for FY 2023-2024 and FY 2025 (SAFE Reports)
National Strategy for Financial Literacy 2020 provides the framework for federal financial literacy priorities
Current topics include financial capability, saving, investing, consumer credit access, and student lending
why_it_matters: This page provides no market-moving or macro-relevant information. It is an informational resource page describing ongoing government consumer education programs with no direct relevance to market structure, trading operations, or macroeconomic catalysts that would affect financial markets.
